acara_description: Identify corresponding, alternate and co-interior angle relationships for parallel lines cut by a transversal; solve problems.

questions.stem: When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, corresponding angles are:

default: Corresponding angles are on the same side of the transversal.
They form an F-shape (or backwards F).
Corresponding angles in parallel lines are always equal.

questions.worked_solution: Corresponding angles (F-angles) in parallel lines are equal.

questions.stem: Alternate angles in parallel lines are:

default: Alternate angles are on opposite sides of the transversal.
They form a Z-shape (or S-shape).
Alternate angles in parallel lines are always equal.

questions.worked_solution: Alternate angles (Z-angles) in parallel lines are equal.

questions.stem: Co-interior angles (same-side interior angles) in parallel lines add up to:

default: Co-interior angles are between the parallel lines, on the same side of the transversal.
They form a C-shape (or U-shape).
Co-interior angles are supplementary.

questions.worked_solution: Co-interior angles (C-angles) in parallel lines add to 180°.

questions.stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One co-interior angle is 65°. Find the other co-interior angle in degrees.

default: Co-interior angles add to 180°.

questions.stem: A corresponding angle is 72°. Find the corresponding angle on the parallel line.

default: Corresponding angles in parallel lines are equal.
The angle equals 72°.

questions.stem: An alternate angle is 48°. Find the alternate angle on the other side of the transversal.

default: Alternate angles in parallel lines are equal.
The angle equals 48°.

questions.stem: Parallel lines cut by a transversal. One co-interior angle is 130°. Find the other co-interior angle in degrees.

default: Co-interior angles add to 180°.

questions.stem: Two co-interior angles are x° and 2x°. Find x.

default: Co-interior angles add to 180°.
Corresponding angles in parallel lines are equal.
\(2y=60\;\Rightarrow\;y=30\).

questions.stem: Which two types of angles formed by parallel lines and a transversal are always equal to each other?

default: Recall the rules for each angle type.
Corresponding (F) and alternate (Z) angles are both equal.
Co-interior (C) angles are supplementary (add to 180°), not equal.

questions.worked_solution: Corresponding angles (equal) and alternate angles (equal) — both are equal. Co-interior angles add to 180°.
